description: Model x granularity sweep: the none/fine/medium/coarse toolsets across a fixed-size (7x7) difficulty ladder, run on a capability ladder of models (Haiku 4.5 < Sonnet 4.6 < Opus 4.7 anchor), meta-off, n>=3 per cell.
motivation: Test H-MG: whether the difficulty rung where granularity helps shifts with model capability. Completes the a-0001 model x granularity pillar, never tested before.
formal_results: (planned — no trials yet) Analysis via harness/analyze_model_x_granularity.py: per (model,puzzle,toolset) cost mu+-sigma, benefit = 1 - cheapest/none, 1-sigma separation test, and the MG1 monotonicity verdict.
methodology: Models: claude-haiku-4-5, claude-sonnet-4-6, and the existing Opus-4.7 baseline reused as the strong anchor (--include-default). Fixed 7x7 board size so difficulty (search_nodes) varies independent of size; ladder = two generated low rungs + puzzle_002 (~501) + gen_7x7_s3_00 (3789) + gen_7x7_s1_00 (4547); puzzle_004 (15x15) as the shared ceiling for MG3. n>=3, meta-off, same 1-sigma overlap protocol as Phase-1/2. Resumable, window-budget-capped, model-aware resume counting; live runs gated on BUDGET_AUTHORIZED. Weaker models are cheaper (~0.6x Sonnet, ~0.25x Haiku of Opus).
report:
## Planned: model x granularity sweep

**Goal.** Decide H-MG ([a-0014]): does the granularity Goldilocks zone move with model capability?

**Code.** `harness/run_model_grid.py` (resumable, model-aware, budget-gated) + `harness/analyze_model_x_granularity.py` (groups by model x puzzle x toolset, computes the benefit surface, tests MG1). Design note: `notes/2026-06-09_model_x_granularity.md`.

**Grid.** models {Haiku 4.5, Sonnet 4.6, Opus 4.7-anchor} x ladder {2 low 7x7 rungs, puzzle_002, gen_7x7_s3_00, gen_7x7_s1_00} x toolsets {none,fine,medium,coarse}, n>=3, meta-off. The Opus anchor reuses existing baseline trials, so only Sonnet/Haiku cells cost new money.

**Run.**
```
python -m harness.run_model_grid --dry-run --include-default ...   # free plan
export BUDGET_AUTHORIZED=1
python -m harness.run_model_grid --include-default ... --window-budget 8
python -m harness.analyze_model_x_granularity
```

**Read-out.** MG1: peak-benefit difficulty non-decreasing with capability. MG2: granularity separates from `none` on an easier rung for weaker models. MG3: solve-rate cliff lower for weaker models.
